layout.responsive: Full width/height on mobile, Windowed on desktop

TerminalWindow.description: The main container. Looks like a clean macOS/Linux terminal window.
TerminalWindow.styles: bg-[#0D0D0D] border border-[#333333] rounded-lg shadow-2xl overflow-hidden flex flex-col font-mono text-[#E0E0E0] relative
TerminalWindow.header: Mac-style buttons (monochrome/dimmed) or simple text title 'guest@anushka-portfolio:~'

CommandInput.styles: bg-transparent border-none outline-none text-[#E0E0E0] w-full caret-transparent

TypewriterEffect.description: Animates text output character by character.

images.usage: Use in 'whoami' command output, styled as a small, pixelated or grayscale ASCII-adjacent image

commands_definition.whoami: Displays profile photo + short bio (Product Marketing Manager).
commands_definition.log: Simulated 'git log' style history of career highlights.
commands_definition.contact: Outputs clickable links for Email and LinkedIn.

instructions_to_main_agent: Store terminal history in a state array: [{ command: 'help', output: <Component/> }, ...]
Implement a custom hook `useTypewriter` for the text animation.
Ensure the input field is always focused when the user clicks anywhere in the terminal.
Support 'ArrowUp' / 'ArrowDown' for command history navigation (like real bash).
Use Tailwind CSS for all styling.
Do NOT use heavy external UI libraries for the terminal logic; build it from scratch using React state for authenticity.
Ensure 'data-testid' attributes are present on the input and output blocks.
For the 'whoami' command, render the profile image using a standard tag with a grayscale filter (`grayscale`) to match the monochrome aesthetic, max width 150px.
UNIVERSAL_GUIDELINES_FOR_MAIN_AGENT: Use 'sonner' for any system notifications if needed (e.g., 'Copied to clipboard').
